message.content:
You are doing reverse-engineering reconnaissance on the decompiled source of Tyranny (Obsidian 2016, Unity 5.4 Mono, engine shared with Pillars of Eternity 1). The decompiled C# source (from ILSpy over Assembly-CSharp.dll) is at `H:\RL\tools\extracted\assembly_csharp\` — 1147 .cs files. Use Grep/Read to find exact member signatures, then WRITE your findings to `H:\RL\tools\decompile_notes.md`.

Purpose: we are building a BepInEx 5 / HarmonyX plugin that (a) reads game state, (b) injects virtual player input, (c) intercepts dialogue option text before render, (d) triggers save/load and new-game programmatically, (e) controls game speed. Your notes are the reference every Harmony hook will be written from. Precision matters: record EXACT class names, method signatures (visibility, static/instance, parameter types, return types), field/property names, and the file you found them in.

Investigate these 12 areas. For each, record the exact members + a one-line "hookability verdict" (e.g. "postfix-patchable", "read via static singleton", "needs virtual-input override"):

1. **GameState** (GameState.cs): static singleton/instance access; combat flag (InCombat or similar); paused state; current map/area name or id; game-over/death state; loading state.
2. **Conversation system** (ConversationManager.cs, Conversation.cs, FlowChartPlayer.cs, and anything like ConversationData): how the active conversation is accessed; current node id; the method that enumerates the player's response options (links from question nodes); how option display text is resolved (StringTable lookup path); the method called when the player picks response index i.
3. **Dialogue UI** (search for classes setting option text — likely UI classes referencing conversation, e.g. UIConversation*, UIDialog*; the engine uses NGUI so look for UILabel usage): the component/method that builds the on-screen response list (patch point for text swap + order shuffle); how a click or number-key maps to a response index.
4. **Reputation/factions** (Reputation.cs, ReputationManager.cs, Faction.cs, FactionData if present): per-faction Favor/Wrath storage; the mutator method(s) that add reputation points (our postfix hook = favor event stream); how factions are identified (enum/string ids like SK_GravenAshe); how to READ current favor/wrath values.
5. **Quests** (QuestManager.cs and related): methods that advance quest objectives / set end states (postfix hook = milestone events); how quest names/ids are exposed.
6. **Global variables** (GlobalVariables.cs): getter/setter signatures (setter postfix = event stream).
7. **Time** (WorldTime.cs, TimeController.cs): current in-game day/time counter (for the Edict countdown); TimeScaleMultiplier property; where UnityEngine.Time.timeScale gets written.
8. **Save/load** (GameResources.cs, SaveGameInfo.cs, anything with SaveGame/LoadGame): programmatic save and load methods; the function that computes the save directory (patch point to redirect saves per instance); how loading completion can be detected.
9. **Input** (GameInput.cs, GameCursor.cs, and NGUI's UICamera.cs if present in the decompile): how world-input is read (which UnityEngine.Input calls, or central wrapper methods/properties we can prefix-patch to inject virtual cursor position, mouse buttons, and keys); UICamera input delegates/hooks (this NGUI version may expose UICamera.GetKeyDown / onCustomInput static delegates); how the game maps screen position to world clicks.
10. **Character creation & level-up UI** (UICharacterCreationManager.cs, level-up related classes): visibility/active flags usable for mode detection; whether creation screens are driven by normal cursor+click; any default character name behavior (is a name text-entry mandatory?).
11. **New game / main menu** (search MainMenu, NewGame): programmatic path to start a new game from the main menu, and to return to main menu from in-game (for episode resets).
12. **Debug console** (search for console command registration, iroll20s, CommandLine): the command dispatcher class and how to invoke commands programmatically (e.g. reputationaddpoints) for testing.

Also note anything you incidentally find about: Edict/timer mechanics ("DayOfSwords", "Edict"), area transition events, party member enumeration (PartyMemberAI), and per-character health (Health.cs, CharacterStats.cs) — HP fields, position access (transform), selection state.

Write `H:\RL\tools\decompile_notes.md` structured with one section per area: exact signatures in code blocks, source file references, hookability verdict, and any surprises/risks. Be exhaustive but organized — this file is the single reference for all future hook work. Do NOT copy large code bodies; signatures + brief behavioral notes only. Do not run any git commands.

- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). # Tyranny Decompile Notes — Harmony Hook Reference

Source: ILSpy decompile of `Assembly-CSharp.dll` at `H:\RL\tools\extracted\assembly_csharp\` (1147 .cs).
Game: Tyranny (Obsidian 2016), Unity 5.4 Mono, OEI/PoE1 shared engine. NGUI UI.

## CRITICAL ARCHITECTURE NOTE — read first

The decompiled assembly contains only two layers:
- **`Game.*` namespace** (files in `Game/` subdir): the game-specific subclasses, e.g. `Game.GameState : SDK.GameState`, `Game.Reputation : SDK.Reputation`, `Game.WorldTime : SDK.WorldTime`, `Game.GameResources : SDK.GameResources`, `Game.CommandLine : SDK.CommandLine`, etc.
- **Global-namespace UI classes** (files in root): `UIConversationManager`, `UIMainMenuManager`, `UICommandLine`, `UICharacterCreationManager`, etc.

**The entire `SDK.*` base layer is in a SEPARATE assembly that was NOT decompiled here** (likely `Assembly-CSharp-firstpass.dll` / an OEI SDK dll). `grep "namespace SDK"` returns 0 hits. This means:
- Many core engine types have **no source here at all**: `SDK.GameInput`, `SDK.Console`, `SDK.GlobalVariables`, `SDK.TimeController`, `SDK.PersistenceManager`, `SDK.SaveGameInfo`, `OEIFormats.FlowCharts.Conversations.Conversation`, `ConversationManager`, `FlowChartPlayer`, `PlayerResponseNode`, `SDK.CharacterStats`, `SDK.Health`, `SDK.PartyMemberAI`, `SDK.Faction`, `SDK.QuestManager` base.
- All signatures below marked **[SDK, inferred]** were reconstructed from *usage* in the decompiled Game-layer code. Member names/params are reliable (they compile against the real DLL), but you should confirm exact overload/visibility against the shipped `Assembly-CSharp-firstpass.dll` with dnSpy/ILSpy before finalizing a patch. Types marked **[HERE]** are fully decompiled in this assembly.
- For Harmony: you can `AccessTools.TypeByName("SDK.GameState")` etc. at runtime regardless of which DLL — the CLR resolves across assemblies. Patch targets in the SDK layer are fully reachable; you just can't read their bodies here.

Namespaces are file-scoped (`namespace Game;`). `using SDK;` means unqualified `GameInput`, `Console`, `GlobalVariables`, `TimeController` in Game-layer code all resolve to `SDK.*`.

---

## 1. GameState  — `Game/GameState.cs` [HERE], base `SDK.GameState` [SDK]

`public class Game.GameState : SDK.GameState` (a MonoBehaviour singleton).

**Singleton / player access:**
```csharp
public new static GameState Instance => SDK.GameState.s_instance as GameState;   // [HERE]
public new static Player s_playerCharacter { get; set; }   // [HERE] override wrapper; base SDK field SDK.GameState.s_playerCharacter (a CharacterStats-ish); here returns Game.Player
protected static SDK.GameState.s_instance                  // [SDK] backing field, MonoBehaviour
public static event EventHandler PlayerCharacterChanged;    // [HERE]
```
`GameState.Instance` is the go-to accessor. `GameState.s_playerCharacter` is `Game.Player` (has `.gameObject`, `.Character` (CharacterStats), `.GetComponent<...>()`).

**Combat flag** (all [SDK] statics, set inside `Game.GameState.UpdateIsInCombatAndGameOver()` [HERE]):
```csharp
SDK.GameState.InCombat        // static bool property (public getter)  -> READ combat state
SDK.GameState.s_isInCombat    // static bool backing field
public static bool CannotSaveBecauseInCombat => SDK.GameState.s_isInCombat || s_noSaveInCombatCountdown > 0;  // [HERE]
public static bool IsInTrapTriggeredCombat { get; }  // [HERE]
public static float InCombatDuration => s_inCombatTimer;  // [HERE]
public static bool ForceCombatMode { get; set; }  // [HERE] force combat on
```
Combat start/end raise `SDK.GameState.OnCombatStart` / `OnCombatEnd` (EventHandler events) — good postfix/subscribe points. `UpdateIsInCombatAndGameOver()` runs every `Update()`.

**Paused state:** owned by `TimeController` (see §7), NOT GameState. `SDK.GameState.Paused` [SDK] static bool exists (used in `UpdateIsInCombatAndGameOver`: `if (SDK.GameState.Paused ...) return;`) and mirrors TimeController pause.

**Current map / area:**
```csharp
public MapData GetCurrentMap();  public void SetCurrentMap(MapData);   // [HERE] instance
public MapData GetLastMap();  public MapData GetCurrentNextMap();
public Region CurrentRegion { get; }                 // [HERE] current world Region
public override string GetCurrentMapName();          // [HERE] returns MapData.GetDisplayNameText()
public bool CurrentMapIsStronghold { get; }
SDK.GameState.LoadedLevelName   // [SDK] static string — the scene name currently loaded
SDK.GameState.ApplicationLoadedLevelName  // [SDK] static string
```

**Game-over / death:**
```csharp
SDK.GameState.GameOver / SDK.GameState.s_gameOver   // [SDK] static bool (s_gameOver set true when PartyDead)
SDK.GameState.PartyDead                              // [SDK] static bool
public static bool CutsceneAllowed => !SDK.GameState.PartyDead && !SDK.GameState.GameOver;  // [HERE]
```
On game over, `UIDeathManager.Instance.ShowWindow()` is called ~2s after PartyDead.

**Loading state:**
```csharp
SDK.GameState.IsLoading         // [SDK] static bool — true during level transition, set false in FinalizeLevelLoad()
SDK.GameState.IsTransitionInProgress  // [SDK] static bool
SDK.GameState.IsRestoredLevel   // [SDK] static bool
SDK.GameState.LoadedGame / NewGame / FirstPlaythrough  // [SDK] static bool flags
SDK.GameState.NumSceneLoads     // [SDK] static int
public void FinalizeLevelLoad();  // [HERE] fires OnLevelLoaded* events, sets IsLoading=false — HOOK for "load complete"
```

**Scene transition helpers [HERE, static]:** `LoadMainMenu(bool fadeOut)`, `ChangeLevel(MapData/MapType/string)`, `LoadLevel(MapData/string)`, `BeginLevelUnload(string)`, `Autosave()`, `EndGameAndLoadCredits()`, `ReturnToMainMenuFromError()`.

**Difficulty / mode:** `public static GameMode Mode;` and `public static GameMode Option => Mode;` [HERE]. `Difficulty` (GameDifficulty), `TrialOfIron`, `ExpertMode` persistent props.

**Verdict:** Read combat/loading/gameover via `SDK.GameState` static props (`InCombat`, `IsLoading`, `GameOver`, `Paused`). Subscribe to `OnCombatStart/OnCombatEnd` events. `GameState.Instance` for map/region. Postfix `FinalizeLevelLoad` = "area load finished" event. All read-via-static-singleton; no virtual override needed.

---

## 2. Conversation system  [SDK / OEIFormats, inferred] + `UIConversationManager` [HERE]

Core flow classes live in `OEIFormats.FlowCharts` / `OEIFormats.FlowCharts.Conversations` / `SDK` (NOT decompiled here). All signatures below reconstructed from `UIConversationManager.cs` and `GameState.cs`/`GameResources.cs` usage.

**Conversation** (`OEIFormats.FlowCharts.Conversations.Conversation`) [SDK] — key methods used by UI:

**Key patch seams captured (exact signatures + file refs in the notes):**
- **State:** `SDK.GameState.InCombat/IsLoading/GameOver/Paused` statics; `OnCombatStart/OnCombatEnd` events; postfix `GameState.FinalizeLevelLoad` for load-complete.

- **Globals:** `GlobalVariables.Instance.GetVariable(string)→int` / `SetVariable(string,int)`.
- **Time:** `TimeController.Instance` (Paused/Slow/Fast); WorldTime calendar for the Edict countdown.
- **Save/load:** `GameResources.SaveGame/LoadGame` (static); redirect saves by patching `SDK.GameResources.SaveGamePath` getter.

### Networking check (coordinator long-shot) — RESOLVED: nothing unusual
Searched both assemblies for `TcpListener` / `System.Net.Sockets` / raw `Socket` use: **zero** in game code. Only network-adjacent code is Steamworks (`Steamworks/SteamNetworking.cs`, `SteamGameServerNetworking.cs` — Steam P2P API, not OS sockets) and one `using System.Net` in `Game/FeedProvider_Paradox.cs` (HTTP news feed). No socket hooking, no Detours-style interception, nothing that would make a `TcpListener.Start()` lie. If your plugin's `TcpListener.Start()` succeeds but the port is invisible in netstat, suspect the **environment, not the game**: Unity 5.4 ships an ancient Mono 2.x-era runtime whose `TcpListener` defaults can bind IPv6-only (check `netstat -ano` for `[::]:port` not just `0.0.0.0:port`), or the listener object was GC'd/closed before you checked. Verify from inside the process via `IPGlobalProperties.GetIPGlobalProperties().GetActiveTcpListeners()` and bind explicitly to `IPAddress.Any` (or `IPAddress.Loopback`) with the port, then keep a strong reference.

### Networking check (TcpListener long-shot) — RESOLVED: nothing unusual in game code
Searched both assemblies for `TcpListener` / `System.Net.Sockets` / raw `Socket` usage: **zero hits** in game code. The only network-adjacent code is Steamworks (`firstpass/Steamworks/SteamNetworking.cs`, `SteamGameServerNetworking.cs` — Steam P2P API over the Steam client, not OS sockets) and one `using System.Net` in `Game/FeedProvider_Paradox.cs` (HTTP news feed). No socket hooking, no interception, nothing that would make `TcpListener.Start()` succeed without a real bind. If a plugin's listener port is invisible in netstat, suspect the **runtime/environment, not the game**: Unity 5.4 ships an ancient Mono 2.x-era class library whose `TcpListener` can end up bound IPv6-only (check `netstat -ano` for `[::]:port`, not just `0.0.0.0:port`), or the listener was GC'd/closed. Verify from inside the process with `IPGlobalProperties.GetIPGlobalProperties().GetActiveTcpListeners()`, construct with explicit `new TcpListener(IPAddress.Any /* or Loopback */, port)`, and hold a strong reference.

## Report: SDK verification summary

Every `[SDK, inferred]` signature in `H:\RL\tools\decompile_notes.md` was checked against `H:\RL\tools\extracted\assembly_csharp_firstpass\` and the file was rewritten in place with `[verified]` markers, file/line refs, and a corrected cheat-sheet. Corrections found:

**Namespace correction (affects every Harmony `TypeByName` call):** `GameInput`, `GlobalVariables`, `TimeController`, `Console`, `Conversation`, `ConversationManager`, `FlowChart`, `FlowChartPlayer`, `SaveGameInfo`, `PersistenceManager` are **global-namespace** classes in firstpass, not `SDK.*`. Only `SDK.GameState/GameResources/CommandLine/Reputation/QuestManager/PartyMemberAI/Health/GameCursor/WorldTime/CharacterStats` are in `namespace SDK`. Node data classes (`PlayerResponseNode`, `FlowChartNode`, `DialogueNode`) live in a third assembly, `OEIFormats.dll` (still not decompiled; no planned hook targets them directly).

**GameInput (virtual-input seam, verified whole file):** `NumberPressed` is **-1** when idle (not 0); `ClickHandled` is a **set-only** property; `GetKey(KeyCode)` bypasses `DisableInput`; all `MappedControl` overloads funnel into `GetControlDown/Up(KeyControl,bool)` and `GetControl(KeyControl,bool,bool)` — the single choke point to prefix. Critical: `GameInput.Update()` reads raw `Input.mousePosition` for the world raycast, so prefixing `MousePosition` alone won't move the world pick — instead postfix `GameInput.Update` and write `GameCursor.WorldPickPosition`/`CharacterUnderCursor`, both confirmed **publicly settable**.

**GameState:** the events are `CombatStart/CombatEnd/LevelUnload/LevelLoaded[Early|Late]/Resting` (my notes had the raiser names `OnCombat*`); `Paused` delegates to `TimeController.Paused` (`Time.timeScale == 0f`); `s_playerCharacter` is a public static **field** of type `Player`.

**TimeController (big win):** `public void Flash(float speed)` is a **public arbitrary-multiplier API**; `NormalTime/SlowTime/FastTime` are public instance fields you can just overwrite; private `UpdateTimeScale()` is the sole `Time.timeScale` writer and stomps external writes every frame (postfix it if forcing values).

**Conversation stack:** `GetNodeText` has a 4th defaulted param (`NodeTextRequestType { Unspecified, PassingOnly, FailingOnly }`); `PassesConditionalsEx` takes `FlowChartNode`; `MoveToNode/GetNextNode/GetNode` are on the `FlowChart` base; `FlowChartPlayerAdded` and all four `QuestManager.OnQuest*` "events" are actually public **delegate fields** (use `Delegate.Combine`). Bonus: `ConversationManager.StartConversation(...)` verified for programmatic dialogue starts.

**Save redirect:** `SDK.GameResources.SaveGamePath` static getter confirmed (Windows: `Saved Games\Tyranny`) — but full per-instance isolation also requires rewriting `PersistenceManager.s_tempSavePath/s_mobileObjPath/s_oldTempSavePath` (public static fields hardcoded to `persistentDataPath/CurrentGame`) and patching `GlobalVariables.Write/ReadGlobalsToSaveGame`, which hardcode the same path inline.

**GlobalVariables:** `GetVariable` returns **-1** (not 0) for missing keys; `SetVariable(string,int)` verified and already fans out through `QuestManager.TriggerGlobalVariableEvent(string,int)` — an alternative single hook point. Thread-safe `QueueVariable` exists.

**CommandLine.RunCommand(string):** verified; reflects over `Game.CommandLine` always but `Game.Scripts` **only when `SDK.GameState.CheatsEnabled`** — so `reputationaddpoints` (lives in Scripts.cs) requires enabling cheats first.

**Networking long-shot:** resolved — zero `TcpListener`/`System.Net.Sockets` usage in either assembly; only Steamworks P2P and one HTTP news fetcher. Nothing in game code explains a silent listener; the notes point at Unity 5.4's ancient Mono (possible IPv6-only bind — check `[::]:port` in netstat) and in-process verification via `GetActiveTcpListeners()`.
